## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On April 12, 1961, Yuri Gagarin, a massive, incredibly courageous 27-year-old Soviet cosmonaut, achieved the absolute, most monumental milestone in human history. Launching aboard the massive Vostok 1 spacecraft, he became the absolute very first human being to journey into outer space and successfully complete a massive orbital flight around the Earth. This massive technological triumph aggressively cemented absolute Soviet dominance in the early Space Race, profoundly shocking the United States and global scientific community.

On April 12, 1945, US President Franklin D. Roosevelt, the massive, highly iconic architect of the New Deal and the absolute leader of the American war effort during World War II, suddenly and tragically passed away from a massive cerebral hemorrhage in Warm Springs, Georgia. His massive, incredibly unprecedented four-term presidency fundamentally reshaped the American executive branch. His death aggressively elevated Vice President Harry S. Truman to the presidency during the absolute, highly volatile closing months of the global conflict.

On April 12, 1801, Maharaja Ranjit Singh, the massive, highly brilliant, and incredibly formidable military leader, officially and aggressively captured the city of Lahore. He aggressively unified the deeply fractured Sikh misls and fiercely defended his massive northwestern frontiers against aggressive Afghan invasions and the expanding British East India Company, cementing his legacy as the "Lion of Punjab."

On April 12, 2006 (Historical context note), Rajkumar, the absolute, massive, and universally revered cultural icon and superstar of Kannada cinema, passed away in Bangalore.
